analysis: The back half of this stretch is genuinely doing something — four nights in a row at 7h 19m or better, with awake time collapsing from that rough 1h 47m on the 1st down to under 45 minutes consistently, and that 2h 34m REM on the 3rd is the kind of number that makes everything feel more like a person. The earlier part of the stretch was scrappier — two nights under 6h 15m and that brutal almost-two-hours awake on the 1st — so whatever clicked around midweek, you should know it clicked. Deep sleep has been drifting a little low across the board, mostly in that 28–36 minute range lately when it was touching 49–51m at the start, so that's the one thing worth watching without stressing about. But honestly? The trend line on this is pointing the right direction and your REM is being very generous to you.
